description: Short humorous shopkeeper and fisherman dialogue lines for Patient Angler.

normalGreetings: Fresh deals! Slightly fishy prices.
Welcome back, coin collector.
Need gear or just admiring my shelves?
Everything here is waterproof. Mostly.
Step right up! No refunds if it bites.
I polished the rods. The fish remain unimpressed.
Buy now before the crabs unionize.
I sell quality gear and questionable advice.
Looking for upgrades or emotional support?
The bait is fresh. The jokes are not.
Welcome! Try not to spend responsibly.
Coins in, fishing empire out.
I had a sale yesterday. You missed it. Maybe.
New stock! Same smell.
If it breaks, blame the lake.
I accept coins, diamonds, and compliments.
One rod upgrade away from greatness.
The fish asked me not to sell you this.
Careful, these prices are almost legal.
Welcome to the finest shop near this puddle.
I found these upgrades floating downstream.
Spend wisely. Or loudly. I enjoy both.
Every great angler starts by overpaying.
This shop is 90% wood and 10% ambition.
The nets are trained. The boats are moody.
I tested the bait myself. Bad idea.
Need something shiny for your fishing problem?
My gear catches fish. Eventually.
Step inside! The smell means quality.
Business is booming. Unlike that old naval mine.

quickReturnGreetings.message: Back already? Did the door hit you twice?
That was fast. Forgot to buy half the shop?

longAbsenceGreetings.messageTemplate: Gone for {timeAway}? I was starting to blame a sea monster.
{timeAway} away! I almost sold your usual bait to a gull.
After {timeAway}, you return! The fish filed a missing angler report.

implementationNotes.selectionRule: When opening shop: if time since last shop visit < 10 seconds, randomly use quickReturnGreetings. Else if >= 5 real hours, randomly use longAbsenceGreetings and replace {timeAway}. Otherwise randomly use normalGreetings.

globalRules.frequency: At most once per in-game hour per visible fishing screen.
globalRules.style: Short, humorous one-liners from the fisherman NPC.

river: River fish are slippery. River socks are worse.
I asked the current for advice. It kept moving.
If you catch my lunch, please negotiate nicely.
This river knows secrets. Mostly where I dropped my hook.
I saw a boot swim by. Very suspicious.
The fish here respect patience and fear bread.
A calm river hides dramatic fish.
If the line pulls hard, pull harder. Unless it's a log.
The river paid me in splashes again.
lake: The lake is calm. Too calm. Probably plotting.
I once fought a carp for three minutes. We are friends now.
Big fish live deep. My snacks do too.
If you hear bubbles, pretend it's wisdom.
The lake keeps everything. Especially bicycles.
I trust the bobber more than most people.
Some say there is a monster here. I say it owes me bait.
The catfish blinked first. I think.
Never underestimate a fish with free time.
bay: The bay smells like profit and questionable seaweed.
Saltwater fish act fancy until they meet a net.
I waved at a crab. It filed a complaint.
If the tide takes your bait, charge it rent.
Bay fishing: half skill, half arguing with waves.
A gull judged my technique. Fair, honestly.
Somewhere out there, a fish is making bad decisions.
I caught a rope once. Strong personality.
The wind says buy better gear. Convenient.
The bay gives, the bay takes, the bay splashes your boots.
